prompt_template:
Generate a Drupal tip for category #{cat_id}: {cat_desc}

NOTE: When reference data is provided below, your tip MUST be based on specific information from that reference. Extract a concrete, testable tip from the reference content. Do not generate generic tips.

Requirements:
- One paragraph maximum (2-4 sentences)
- Factual, specific, and useful for an experienced Drupal developer
- No padding or filler
- Prefer Drupal 11 / PHP 8.4+ idioms for code examples
- If the tip involves code, include it in a code block with appropriate syntax highlighting (php, bash, sql, yaml, twig, json, etc. based on context)

Output format:
---
category: {cat_name}
title: [Short descriptive title]
---

[The tip paragraph here]

```
[Code example if relevant - choose appropriate language: php/bash/sql/yaml/twig/json]
```

Generate ONE tip now.

2.name: drupal-history
2.desc: Drupal history or very old versions

4.name: drupal-statistics
4.desc: Interesting statistics about Drupal usage, websites using, installation counts, code lines, commits per month, people on the community etc

6.name: high-issue-count-module
6.desc: Module with unusually high issue count

7.name: old-maintained-module
7.desc: Very old but still actively maintained module

10.name: composer-trick
10.desc: Rare Composer command or config trick

11.name: useful-composer-package
11.desc: Useful Composer package for Drupal and PHP development and automation

15.name: hot-issue
15.desc: Hot or important open Drupal.org issue

16.name: release-cycle
16.desc: Drupal release cycle or supported version fact

18.name: llm-prompt-idea
18.desc: Useful LLM prompt or skill idea for Drupal

22.name: web-tip
22.desc: Recent Drupal tip from web search

23.name: ide-tip
23.desc: Recent PHPStorm or VSCode tip for Drupal

27.name: case-study
27.desc: Nice Drupal showcase/case study from the list here. Do not return any code

34.name: core-bash-commands
34.desc: Drupal core bash commands for testing/linting

40.name: plugin-type
40.desc: Explanation of a core plugin type

50.name: coding-standard
50.desc: Drupal coding standard rule often violated

51.name: phpunit-pattern
51.desc: PHPUnit testing pattern specific to Drupal

67.name: module-weight
67.desc: Module weight and hook execution order

70.name: node-access
70.desc: Node access hooks vs entity access handlers

74.name: entity-query
74.desc: Entity query vs direct DB query

78.name: csrf-protection
78.desc: CSRF protection in forms and routes

79.name: mysql
79.desc: MySQL specific queries, best practices and performance tips

81.name: jokes
81.desc: A short joke using Drupal terminology

85.name: rare-devel-command
85.desc: Rare and interesting devel module drush commands

86.name: drupalorg-cli
86.desc: Rare and interesting commands and usages of drupalorg cli

87.name: robo-cli
87.desc: Rare and interesting commands and usages of robo cli

88.name: song
88.desc: Rephrase a famous song versus for Drupal

89.name: webform-module
89.desc: Interesting tips and usages of the webform module and its submodules

90.name: xdebug-xhprof
90.desc: Interesting tips, code examples, cli commands and IDE plugins for xdebug and xhprof with PHP
